title: Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design
summary: Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design retrieves prior skills in an original independent survey redesign release decision scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply design user goals, design information hierarchy, design spacing alignment, design color typography, design consistency feedback, design prototype test in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent survey redesign release decision artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey redesign here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the survey design system here. */

steps.title: 01 · inspect 1: community survey redesign discovery interview · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent survey redesign release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent survey redesign release decision.

content.title: independent survey redesign release decision evidence
content.text: For Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design, choose the design user goals claim that preserves the independent survey redesign release decision purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent survey redesign release decision case 1: the stakeholder feature list is equivalent to evidence about user needs.

Independent survey redesign release decision case 1: separate requests from research evidence, state prioritized user tasks and constraints, and define observable success before choosing interface features.
Independent survey redesign release decision case 1: treat every requested feature as equally urgent so no stakeholder preference is challenged.

options.text: independent survey redesign release decision phase 2.1: rank content and actions by the design brief
independent survey redesign release decision phase 2.2: create structural order and heading relationships
independent survey redesign release decision phase 2.3: apply restrained scale contrast grouping and whitespace
independent survey redesign release decision phase 2.4: run find-and-act tasks with visual keyboard and reading-order checks

Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 3: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent survey redesign release decision case 3: whitespace is unused room that should be filled to maximize value.
Independent survey redesign release decision case 3: map repeated relationships to a small spacing scale, align related edges, and test grouping at narrow width, zoom, and changed content length.
Independent survey redesign release decision case 3: choose a unique margin for each element so every section has a custom personality.

options.text: Independent survey redesign release decision case 4: a brand color must be used for text even when it fails contrast.

Independent survey redesign release decision case 4: choose type and color roles from reading and state needs, adjust brand usage for contrast, and test real content across zoom, themes, and forced colors.
Independent survey redesign release decision case 4: set long-form survey guidance in all caps to strengthen the brand voice.

hints: Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 5: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 5: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 5: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent survey redesign release decision case 5: consistency means every component must look identical regardless of purpose.
Independent survey redesign release decision case 5: standardize patterns by purpose and state, preserve meaningful distinctions, and verify immediate feedback plus recovery across the complete task.
Independent survey redesign release decision case 5: give every message the same blue treatment so users learn one universal state color.

hints: Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 6: identify the user task and data relationship first.

Mastery Exam: User-Centered Interface Design checkpoint 6: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent survey redesign release decision case 6: a polished mockup is proof that users can complete the intended task.

Independent survey redesign release decision case 6: choose fidelity from the risk being tested, observe representative tasks, record behavior rather than preference alone, and revise the design decision.
Independent survey redesign release decision case 6: ask only whether participants like the colors and treat positive opinions as task success.
